items.title: Keeping Illegal Drugs and Firearms Off the Streets Two separate CPD narcotics investigations concluded this week with th...
items.content_text:
Keeping Illegal Drugs and Firearms Off the Streets
 Two separate CPD narcotics investigations concluded this week with the arrest of three men on multiple drug and weapons charges and the seizure of a substantial amount of illegal drugs and firearms. 
 The first, a long-term CPD narcotics unit investigation involving months of investigative work, assistance from CPD’s Gun Team and Neighborhood Policing officers, a traffic stop, and a search warrant resulted in two arrests and the seizure of nearly 12 lbs of meth, more than 300 grams of fentanyl, ecstasy, oxycodone pills, eight handguns (two stolen), a vehicle, and a substantial amount of drug paraphernalia. 
 The second, a stop of the suspect and subsequent search warrants, led to the arrest of the suspect and the seizure of more than two kilos of cocaine, over a pound of fentanyl, meth, two firearms, a significant amount of cash, a brick press, and more drug paraphernalia. 
 All three suspects are in custody at the Hamilton County Jail.

items.title: Chattanooga! We've got some hot weather headed our way next week! Let’s keep cool and cut energy costs. When the heat in...
items.content_text:
Chattanooga! We've got some hot weather headed our way next week! Let’s keep cool and cut energy costs. When the heat index climbs, try these simple tips to stay comfortable and save energy:
 - Keep blinds and curtains closed to block out the sun
 - Use fans to help circulate air (set ceiling fans to spin counterclockwise)
 - Raise your thermostat a few degrees when you're not home
 - Skip the oven and dryer during peak heat hours
 - Check for air leaks around windows and doors
 Small changes can make a big impact!

items.title: Career Opportunity in #EastRidgeTN! 🚧 The City of East Ridge is seeking a motivated and dependable individual to join o...
items.content_text:
Career Opportunity in #EastRidgeTN! 🚧
 The City of East Ridge is seeking a motivated and dependable individual to join our Street Department team as a full-time Equipment Operator!
 In this role, you'll help maintain and improve our community through a variety of duties including street maintenance, construction projects, mowing, stormwater maintenance, and operating a variety of equipment and vehicles.
 Qualifications:
 ✔️ Must be at least 18 years of age
 ✔️ Valid Driver's License required
 ✔️ High School Diploma or equivalent
 Preferred Experience:
 👏🏼 Stormwater, asphalt, concrete, and mowing operations
 👏🏼 Operating equipment such as a backhoe, mini excavator, and skid steer
 This position requires after-hours availability for emergency calls and the ability to lift up to 50 pounds.
 Join a team dedicated to serving the residents of East Ridge and helping keep our city moving forward!
 💻 Apply online today at www.eastridgetn.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and a Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

items.title: Europe’s current heatwave is a reminder that extreme heat is not only a climate issue. It is a child health, development and urban...
items.content_text:
Europe’s current heatwave is a reminder that extreme heat is not only a climate issue. It is a child health, development and urban policy issue.

Young children are especially vulnerable to heat stress, with risks to their health, development, learning and wellbeing beginning before birth.

In this open-access online masterclass, Dr Lindsey Burghardt, Chief Science Officer at the Center on the Developing Child at Harvard University, speaks with Katie Beck, Policy Fellow at LSE Cities, about the impacts of extreme heat on children’s developing bodies and minds, and the urban policy responses that can help mitigate harm.

The session was presented by the Van Leer Foundation, the Urban95 Academy at LSE and the Center on the Developing Child at Harvard University and is now available free to a wider audience.

📽️ Watch the recording here: https://lnkd.in/eEURdHKD

#ExtremeHeat #Heatwave #UrbanHeat #ChildHealth #EarlyChildhoodDevelopment #UrbanPolicy #ClimateAdaptation #ChildFriendlyCities #Urban95Academy

items.title: Sharing an opportunity for muralists from  AIM Center, Inc.!Call for Professional Mural Artists! 🎨 The AIM Center invit...
items.content_text:
Sharing an opportunity for muralists from  AIM Center, Inc.!
Call for Professional Mural Artists! 🎨
 The AIM Center invites qualified mural artists and artist teams to submit qualifications for the design and installation of a large-scale exterior mural at Espero Chattanooga, a new permanent supportive housing development located at the corner of Main Street and Hawthorne Avenue in Chattanooga's historic Highland Park neighborhood.
 The selected artist will co-create directly with AIM Center members, adults living with serious mental illness (SMI), to develop a mural concept that reflects the spirit of Espero, which means "hope" in Spanish. The artwork should celebrate themes of hope, belonging, recovery, resilience, community, and the transformative impact of safe, affordable housing.
 Artists from Chattanooga and the surrounding Appalachian region (TN, GA,AL), especially those with lived experience of mental illness, are strongly encouraged to apply.
 🗓 Applications must be submitted by Friday, July 24, 2026, at 4:00 PM EST.
 Review the complete Request for Qualifications (RFQ), eligibility requirements, project timeline, budget, and submission instructions here ➡ https://aimcenterinc.org/…/espero-chattanooga-mural-project/
 Questions regarding the Espero Chattanooga Mural Project, artist eligibility, submission requirements, or the RFQ process may be directed to development@aimcenterinc.org.

items.title: Former Red Bank Officer Indicted for Operating Patrol Vehicle Intoxicated Hamilton County, TN
items.content_text:
Former Red Bank Officer Indicted for Operating Patrol Vehicle Intoxicated
 Hamilton County, TN – Hamilton County Sheriff Austin Garrett and Hamilton County District Attorney General Coty Wamp announce the grand jury indictment of former Red Bank Police Officer Brantley Young for Driving Under the Influence and Possession of a Firearm While Under the Influence.
 A joint investigation by the HCSO Criminal Investigations Unit and Traffic Investigations Unit into a January 2026 incident involving a suspicion that Young was operating a patrol vehicle under the influence resulted in grand jurors indicting Young. Young was booked into the Hamilton County Jail & Detention Center early this morning.

When energy and fertiliser costs rise, it affects farmers, agricultural markets, and ultimately consumers.
 The upcoming OECD-FAO #AgOutlook 2026-2035 looks at where agricultural and fish markets are heading over the next 10 years.
 This edition of the Outlook also includes analysis of how the recent surge in energy and fertiliser costs could affect global food security, especially in low-income countries.
 📅 Launching 29 June: https://brnw.ch/21x3FMi
items.content_html:
When energy and fertiliser costs rise, it affects farmers, agricultural markets, and ultimately consumers.
The upcoming OECD-FAO #AgOutlook 2026-2035 looks at where agricultural and fish markets are heading over the next 10 years.
This edition of the Outlook also includes analysis of how the recent surge in energy and fertiliser costs could affect global food security, especially in low-income countries.

items.title: Slovenia’s economy has demonstrated resilience amid external shocks, with its real GDP growing at a rate surpassing that...
items.content_text:
Slovenia’s economy has demonstrated resilience amid external shocks, with its real GDP growing at a rate surpassing that of the OECD and the euro area since 2022.
 Projected GDP growth is set to strengthen to 1.9% in 2026 and 2.2% in 2027, as energy price pressures are assumed to diminish. 
 Inflation is expected to gradually ease by 2027.
 For further insights, refer to the #OECD Economic Survey of Slovenia ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x3Fcv

OMI is scheduled for Wednesday and Thursday, Aug. 19-20, in residential spaces across campus as new students move in. The two-day effort brings together faculty, staff, students and organizations to create a welcoming, supportive environment for incoming students and their families. 💙 
 "At OMI, there is no performance. There are parents trying to keep things moving. There are grandparents cheering from the sidewalk. There are first-generation college students stepping into a new world with family members who may be navigating a college campus for the first time. There are parents dropping off a fourth child and parents dropping off their only child.”
 🔗  Learn more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/people-are-the-thing-operation-move…/

items.title: This grass at Miller Park will look a little blue this October! So excited to host IBMA again this year. One of the many...
items.content_text:
This grass at Miller Park will look a little blue this October! So excited to host IBMA again this year. One of the many things that makes our city a great place to live.
Say hello to Pickin’ in the Park 🎶🌿
 Formerly known as Streetfest, this fan-favorite free festival experience is coming back to IBMA Bluegrass Live!® in 2026, now with a fresh name and a new home in Chattanooga's Miller Park.
 Join us October 23 and 24 for two days of live bluegrass from college bands, Kids on Bluegrass, and local Chattanooga talent, plus arts and crafts vendors, food, and plenty of space to kick back and enjoy the music. No fences, no tickets, just a community gathering to celebrate bluegrass together. 💛
 While Pickin’ in the Park is free and open to all, tickets will be required for the IBMA Bluegrass Live!® indoor performances, and they'll be on sale soon at worldofbluegrass.org. 👀
 Speaking of IBMA Bluegrass Live!®… a quick reminder that 2026 is bringing a new vibe. 🙌 This year’s festival will offer a more intimate, curated experience in the heart of Chattanooga, with main stages at the Chattanooga Convention Center, The Signal, and Barrelhouse Ballroom. Expect a closer connection to the music you love, with a limited number of tickets available.
 More details coming soon. For now, mark your calendars and get ready for an awesome weekend. 🎻
 Photo: Corey Pelton
 #IBMA #IBMA2026 #IBMAWorldofBluegrass #VisitChatt Visit Chattanooga

items.title: 🧡 June is Gun Violence Awareness Month. By the end of today, two women in America will likely be shot and killed by a c...
items.content_text:
🧡 June is Gun Violence Awareness Month.
 By the end of today, two women in America will likely be shot and killed by a current or former intimate partner.
 This is more than a statistic.
 It's two lives cut short.
 Two families forever changed.
 Two communities left grieving.
 Research shows that when an abusive partner has access to a firearm, the risk that a woman will be killed increases dramatically.
 Domestic violence and gun violence are not separate issues. They intersect every day in homes and communities across our country.
 This month, we honor those whose lives have been lost, support survivors, and recommit ourselves to preventing violence before it turns deadly.
 Because safety should never depend on whether an abuser has access to a gun.
 #GunViolenceAwarenessMonth #WearOrange #EndDomesticViolence #DomesticViolenceAwareness #PreventionMatters #TNCoalition #EndTheViolence

items.title: Join us this Sunday, June 28, for CareFest at Shredder's Lair—an all-day market, concert, and community fundraiser benef...
items.content_text:
Join us this Sunday, June 28, for CareFest at Shredder's Lair—an all-day market, concert, and community fundraiser benefiting the Comunidad Care Fund.
 By attending, you'll make a tangible difference for families impacted by immigration enforcement, connect with local organizers and entrepreneurs, discover ways to get involved and enjoy a full day of music, food, art, and community.
 🎵 11 AM–3 PM: Vendor market & free live music
 📢 3–4 PM: Community program on the immigrant justice landscape in Chattanooga and Hamilton County
 🎸 5–10 PM: Rock benefit concert ($10 at the door, with 100% of ticket sales supporting the Care Fund)
 CareFest is built on a simple belief: when systems criminalize and abandon our neighbors, we show up for one another.
 That's why musicians, artists, vendors, volunteers, and local businesses are donating their time, talent and resources to support families in our community.
 Bring a friend. Support local vendors & artists. Meet your neighbors. Help us raise $5,000 for the Comunidad Care Fund.

 We hope to see you there! 🚌
 Learn more by visiting www.gocarta.org.

items.title: HCSO Arrests Man after Vehicle Pursuit with Multiple Warrants and Illegal Items Hamilton County, TN
items.content_text:
HCSO Arrests Man after Vehicle Pursuit with Multiple Warrants and Illegal Items
 Hamilton County, TN – Early this morning on June 25, 2026, an alert was sent out for a stolen vehicle near the intersection of Campbell Street and Glass Street.
 A Chattanooga Police Department officer located the vehicle, and once an HCSO deputy arrived as backup, initiated a traffic stop. The driver fled, initiating a vehicle pursuit that was taken over by the HCSO deputy. The pursuit ended when the vehicle crashed at the 2300 block of East 5th Street.
 The driver, Thomas Williams, was taken into custody by the HCSO, and deputies searched the vehicle, resulting in the seizure of the following:
 • 1 ounce of Methamphetamine
 • 7 ounces of Cocaine
 • One glass pipe
 • $944 in cash
 Williams faces the following charges: 
 • Evading Arrest
 • Reckless Endangerment
 • Theft of Property 
 • Driving While in Possession of Methamphetamine 
 • Drugs General Category for Resale
 • Possession of Controlled Substance
 • Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
 In addition to this incident, Williams was found to have outstanding warrants from the Hamilton County Criminal Court, Hamilton County General Sessions Court, and the East Ridge Police Department.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

• $944 in cash
Williams faces the following charges:
• Evading Arrest
• Reckless Endangerment
• Theft of Property
• Driving While in Possession of Methamphetamine
• Drugs General Category for Resale
• Possession of Controlled Substance
• Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
In addition to this incident, Williams was found to have outstanding warrants from the Hamilton County Criminal Court, Hamilton County General Sessions Court, and the East Ridge Police Department.
This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

 *Admission is FREE!

items.title: The new #OECD Economic Survey of the Slovak Republic highlights the need to strengthen public finances while implementin...
items.content_text:
The new #OECD Economic Survey of the Slovak Republic highlights the need to strengthen public finances while implementing structural reforms to enhance business dynamism and long-term growth.
 The Slovak economy is facing growing pressure from global trade tensions, a weak European car market, a large budget deficit, and its ageing population.
 The Slovak Republic needs to reform retirement rules to extend working lives and encourage female labour market participation through childcare and parental leave reforms.
 Additionally, stronger R&D support and faster AI adoption would boost labour productivity and diversify the car manufacturing base into emerging areas of demand. Reducing regulatory barriers, investing in skills, and accelerating the digital and green transitions are essential for achieving sustainable, inclusive growth.
 Read more: https://brnw.ch/21x3DSw

What an incredible week for our student artists! Last week’s showcase at the Hamilton County Courthouse was a beautiful celebration of creativity and community.
 We want to send a heartfelt thank you to the Hamilton County Courthouse officials for hosting our students and supporting this exhibition. We are also deeply grateful to the families, educators, ArtsBuild board members, and community advocates who came out to walk the gallery and champion these rising talents.
 🖼️ Missed the courthouse exhibit? You're in luck!
 The showcase has officially moved to its next stop. You can view the artwork at Chattanooga State’s Heinley Arts Center from now through July 30th.
 Join us for the grand finale! We will be hosting a Closing Showcase & Artist Reception at Chatt State on Thursday, July 30, from 6:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. Come celebrate the students one last time—plus, artists will be able to take their artwork home with them right after the closing ceremony.
 Check out some moments from last week's courthouse exhibit below! 👇📸
 #ArtsBuild #ChattanoogaState #ChattArts #ArtsEducation #CommunityPartnerships #StudentShowcase

items.content_text:
A huge thank you to our EPB crews and the additional utility workers who worked continuously day and night to restore power following Monday night's severe storms.
 From repairing broken poles and replacing downed lines to navigating difficult conditions after dark, these teams have worked tirelessly to safely restore service to our community. Thanks to their dedication and the support of crews from neighboring utilities, thousands of customers have already had their power restored.
 We're grateful for the hard work, long hours, and commitment these crews show when our community needs them most. We're also incredibly grateful to our customers for your support throughout the restoration process. 💙

items.title: UTC Quantum Summer Camp returned for the second year June 15-18, offering high school students in grades 10 through 12 t...
items.content_text:
UTC Quantum Summer Camp returned for the second year June 15-18, offering high school students in grades 10 through 12 the opportunity to explore the world of quantum through lab tours, talks with professors and scientific demonstrations that bring science to life. 
 UC Foundation Professor of Physics Tatiana Allen emphasized the importance of introducing the next generation to quantum physics and computing.
 “They will see pictures and meet the people, go to EPB, see the quantum computers and see the quantum network,” Allen said. “We showed them some quantum physics experiments. We gave them some demonstrations. It’s a first introduction to physics, math and quantum computing.”
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/quantum-summer-camp-returns-for-sec…/

Join Our Pack in #EastRidgeTN! 🐾
 The City of East Ridge is currently accepting applications for both a full-time and part-time Animal Services Support Staff position with East Ridge Animal Services.
 If you are passionate about animal welfare, enjoy serving the public, and want to make a difference in the lives of shelter animals every day, we encourage you to apply!

 ✅ Must be at least 18 years of age

 ✅ Two or more years of customer service experience required
 ✅ Animal shelter, veterinary, or related experience preferred

 Join a team dedicated to providing compassionate care while creating a clean, safe, and humane environment for shelter animals. 🐶
 📍 Apply online at www.eastridgetn.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

items.title: We are so proud of the bravery and quick action of our Chattanooga Fire Department!MULTIPLE RESCUES MADE BY CFD: Frantic...
items.content_text:
We are so proud of the bravery and quick action of our Chattanooga Fire Department!
MULTIPLE RESCUES MADE BY CFD: Frantic calls to 911 prompted a two-alarm response at a Chattanooga apartment complex late Tuesday night. 
 At 11:29 PM on 6/23/26, Blue Shift companies were dispatched to 404 Tunnel Boulevard after residents reported being trapped in their apartments by heavy smoke. Several of them fled to their balconies to escape the smoke. One family was trapped on their third floor balcony with small children. Due to entrapment, a second alarm was called to bring additional manpower and resources to the scene. Firefighters quickly started making rescues using ladders to get residents down off their balconies while other crews established a water supply, began making interior searches of apartments to look for other residents, and initiated fire attack.
 Firefighters encountered heavy fire conditions and made an aggressive interior attack. The fire was under control by 12:03 AM. Crews conducted extensive checks for fire extension and remaining hot spots. The cause is under investigation.
 In total, nine people and four pets were rescued from Building E. Several apartments sustained damage. As a result of the hard work of firefighters and their well-coordinated response, there were no injuries. 
 Engine 15, Ladder 5, Engine 5, Squad 13, Engine 4, Engine 6, Ladder 6, Squad 1, Engine 1, Engine 10, Engine 8, Ladder 21, Engine 21, Squad 7, Battalion 1, Battalion 2, Battalion 3, and CFD Investigations responded, along with CPD, HCEMS and EPB. 
 Thanks to our Tri-State Mutual Aid partners for filling in and providing coverage at our fire stations while CFD personnel worked this fire. And thanks to our dispatchers for getting and relaying info needed to save lives.

The letter addresses the ADA’s “integration mandate” and the interpretation of the landmark Olmstead decision, raising important questions for the disability community.
 Your voice matters. Read the letter, learn more and contact your elected officials at: https://conta.cc/4aheTIi.

 If you’ve spent any time around Chattanooga, chances are you’ve crossed paths with Juanita. She shows up with a smile, a sense of fun, and a genuine love for this community that’s hard to miss.
 From outreach events to local nonprofits, Juanita is all in, giving her time and energy to the causes that matter most. Whether she’s helping behind the scenes or bringing a little extra joy to an event (as you can see 👀), she reminds us that volunteering can be both meaningful and fun.
 We’re grateful for folks like Juanita who make this community stronger just by showing up.

items.title: Although wildlife may appear cute, feeding them can be dangerous for both you and the animals.  Animal bites are a commo...
items.content_text:
Although wildlife may appear cute, feeding them can be dangerous for both you and the animals. 
 Animal bites are a common source of serious infections and can occur through contact with an infected animal, a contaminated surface, or its habitat.
 Bites and scratches pose a significant risk of illness, including rabies, which primarily spreads through bites or scratches from infected wild animals such as raccoons, skunks, or foxes. 
 To protect yourself, your family, and your pets, it's best to keep a safe distance from wildlife. If you have been bitten by a wild animal you suspect may have rabies, or for more information, please visit health.hamiltontn.org/…/se…/environmental/rabiescontrol.aspx or call (423) 209-8110.

items.title: ArtsBuild Awards $540,000 in Mission Support Grants to 21 Local Arts Nonprofits ArtsBuild is proud to announce the Missi...
items.content_text:
ArtsBuild Awards $540,000 in Mission Support Grants to 21 Local Arts Nonprofits
 ArtsBuild is proud to announce the Mission Support grant recipients for 2026-2027. Receiving a total of $540,000 in grants, these 21 local nonprofits demonstrate a dedication to providing arts programs and fostering vibrant art communities within Chattanooga and Hamilton County, as well as supporting our local economy. Those organizations are:
 • Art 120
 • Association of Visual Arts (AVA)
 • Barking Legs Theater (CoPAC)
 • Be the Change Youth Initiative
 • Bessie Smith Cultural Center
 • Chattanooga Ballet
 • Chattanooga Boys Choir
 • Chattanooga Girls Choir
 • Chattanooga Symphony & Opera
 • Chattanooga Theatre Centre
 • The Chattery
 • Creative Discovery Museum
 • East Lake Expression Engine
 • Houston Museum of Decorative Arts
 • Hunter Museum of American Art
 • The Pop-Up Project
 • Scenic City Clay Arts
 • Seed Theatre
 • Songbirds Foundation
 • SPLASH Arts
 • Stove Works
 ArtsBuild grantees are selected by a panel of local community volunteers who evaluate applications based on each organization’s efforts to expand access to the arts through outreach, inclusion, and diverse programming. ArtsBuild offers a variety of grants throughout the year, and a full list is available at artsbuild.com/grant-making.
 “Each of these organizations helps make our area stronger, more connected, and more vibrant by ensuring that arts are accessible to all,” said Melissa Astin, Director of Grants and Community Engagement at ArtsBuild. “They are committed to ensuring that everyone residing, working, visiting and playing in the Greater Chattanooga area can experience and be inspired by the arts.”
 Mission Support grants are made possible through support from ArtsBuild’s annual donors. ArtsBuild’s community-wide Annual Campaign receives contributions each year from individuals, businesses, foundations and local government, all who believe in the transformative power of arts and culture. Anyone interested in being a part of this work can become an annual donor at artsbuild.com.

items.title: We're proud to share that the Dunlap Bicycle and Pedestrian Plan has received a 2026 Excellence in Regional Transportation Award f...
items.content_text:
We're proud to share that the Dunlap Bicycle and Pedestrian Plan has received a 2026 Excellence in Regional Transportation Award from the National Association of Development Organizations (NADO)

This recognition reflects the power of regional collaboration and community-driven planning. Funded by Tennessee Department of Transportation and administered through SETD's Regional Planning Organization (RPO) Program, the plan established a long-term vision for safer, more connected transportation options for the City of Dunlap.

A special thank you to City of Dunlap Mayor Dwain Land, city leadership, community stakeholders, and residents whose engagement and commitment helped bring this vision to life. Their collaboration played a key role in creating a plan that supports safety, connectivity, tourism, economic development, and future investment opportunities.

We would also like to recognize our outstanding SETD RPO team for their dedication, expertise, and hard work throughout the planning process. This award is a testament to the impact that thoughtful planning and strong partnerships can have on our communities.

Congratulations to everyone involved in this nationally recognized achievement!

items.title: As part of our $2 million Renewal Project, Hamilton County invested opioid settlement dollars in organizations working e...
items.content_text:
As part of our $2 million Renewal Project, Hamilton County invested opioid settlement dollars in organizations working every day to support addiction recovery and build stronger families. Part of that commitment set aside $150,000 to the End School-Age Homelessness Initiative, providing support to HCS families who need housing. In just over a year, we’re seeing fewer public school families without a place to live, which has reduced the chronic absenteeism rate.
 The broader Renewal Project included $500,000 investments in both the Launch Pad and CADAS, $250,000 for Helen Ross McNabb, and $150,000 for Partnership for Families, Children and Adults to expand housing for individuals in recovery. Another $450,000 was split between Chambliss Center for Children and the Boys & Girls Club to strengthen families and serve vulnerable youth. 
 When public resources are entrusted to community partners, the results often exceed what government could achieve on its own. Together, these investments have made Hamilton County a statewide model for innovative strategies in deploying opioid funds in ways that save lives and support vulnerable families.

items.title: Why do so many ambitious public missions lose momentum between announcement and delivery? One answer is that today's urban challen...
items.content_text:
Why do so many ambitious public missions lose momentum between announcement and delivery?

One answer is that today's urban challenges do not sit neatly within departmental boundaries. Delivering change requires city leaders to build coalitions, align institutions and sustain collective effort over time. This is one of the arguments explored by Christian Bason, Ph.D. in a recent Bloomberg Cities Spark magazine.

Bason draws on the experience of Eirik Lae Solberg, Mayor of Oslo and an alum of the first cohort of the Bloomberg LSE European City Leadership Initiative.

Cities participating in the Initiative bring a priority challenge that their mayors, senior leaders and a cross-departmental team address in new ways. For Mayor Solberg, that challenge was youth exclusion, which he says remains a complex and persistent issue even in a well-resourced Nordic city. Alongside challenges relating to public safety and at-risk communities, it is a problem that demands new approaches and collaboration across traditional organisational boundaries.

As Bason describes, Oslo's cross-divisional team is helping to build broader ownership of the city's response, bringing together expertise from across municipal government to shape a new youth policy.

The example highlights an important lesson for city governments: progress on complex challenges rarely depends on a single intervention, department or organisation. It requires leadership that can sustain focus, connect diverse actors and create the conditions for meaningful change over time.

https://lnkd.in/eBZX_PXF

#CityLeadership #UrbanGovernance #CityGovernmentInnovation #UrbanInnovation

Bason draws on the experience of Eirik Lae Solberg, Mayor of Oslo and an alum of the first cohort of the Bloomberg LSE European City Leadership Initiative. Cities participating in the Initiative bring a priority challenge that their mayors, senior leaders and a cross-departmental team address in new ways. For Mayor Solberg, that challenge was youth exclusion, which he says remains a complex and persistent issue even in a well-resourced Nordic city. Alongside challenges relating to public safety and at-risk communities, it is a problem that demands new approaches and collaboration across traditional organisational boundaries. As Bason describes, Oslo's cross-divisional team is helping to build broader ownership of the city's response, bringing together expertise from across municipal government to shape a new youth policy. The example highlights an important lesson for city governments: progress on complex challenges rarely depends on a single intervention, department or organisation. It requires leadership that can sustain focus, connect diverse actors and create the conditions for meaningful change over time. https://lnkd.in/eBZX_PXF #CityLeadership #UrbanGovernance #CityGovernmentInnovation #UrbanInnovation

items.title: Blockchain‑based crypto‑asset transactions – including stablecoins (a type of cryptocurrency designed to keep a stable v...
items.content_text:
Blockchain‑based crypto‑asset transactions – including stablecoins (a type of cryptocurrency designed to keep a stable value, usually by being tied to something else) – have grown the most in Asia among all regions.
 Between 2024 and 2025, crypto-asset transactions increased 69% in Asia - the fastest of all regions.
 This is due to high adoption rates in populous Asian jurisdictions such as India, Indonesia, Viet Nam, the Philippines and Pakistan, and a rapidly growing internet user base and young, tech‑savvy populations.
 What risks does this raise for consumer protection, cyber-security and illicit finance?
 Find out in the #OECDAsiaCapitalMarkets Report ➡️https://brnw.ch/21x3BUN

items.title: MULTIPLE RESCUES MADE BY CFD: Frantic calls to 911 prompted a two-alarm response at a Chattanooga apartment complex late...
items.content_text:
MULTIPLE RESCUES MADE BY CFD: Frantic calls to 911 prompted a two-alarm response at a Chattanooga apartment complex late Tuesday night. 
 At 11:29 PM on 6/23/26, Blue Shift companies were dispatched to 404 Tunnel Boulevard after residents reported being trapped in their apartments by heavy smoke. Several of them fled to their balconies to escape the smoke. One family was trapped on their third floor balcony with small children. Due to entrapment, a second alarm was called to bring additional manpower and resources to the scene. Firefighters quickly started making rescues using ladders to get residents down off their balconies while other crews established a water supply, began making interior searches of apartments to look for other residents, and initiated fire attack.
 Firefighters made an aggressive push to get the fire under control by 12:03 AM. The cause is under investigation.
 In total, nine people were rescued from Building E.  Several apartments sustained damage. As a result of the hard work of firefighters and their well-coordinated response, there were no injuries. 
 Engine 15, Ladder 5, Engine 5, Squad 13, Engine 4, Engine 6, Ladder 6, Squad 1, Engine 1, Engine 10, Engine 8, Ladder 21, Engine 21, Squad 7, Battalion 1, Battalion 2, Battalion 3, and CFD Investigations responded, along with CPD, HCEMS and EPB. 
 Thanks to our Tri-State Mutual Aid partners for filling in and providing coverage at our fire stations while CFD personnel worked this fire. And thanks to our dispatchers for getting and relaying info needed to save lives.

 Do you love bringing people together? 🤝✨ Do you enjoy building relationships, creating opportunities, and making a real difference for children and families? If so, we have an exciting opportunity for you!
 The City of Chattanooga's Department of Early Learning is looking for a Parent, Family & Community Engagement Specialist to join the newly established Office of Parent, Family & Community Engagement!
 This is so much more than a job. It's an opportunity to help shape how we engage families across Chattanooga and make a lasting impact in our community.
 ✨ As our PFCE Specialist, you'll:

 🎉 Help plan and coordinate exciting family programs, signature events and initiatives.
 📚 Lead workshops and trainings.
 💙 Connect families to resources and support that help children thrive.
 🌟 Represent the Department of Early Learning while making a lasting impact every day.
 Why you'll love it:
 ✅ You'll be a direct advocate for local families.
 ✅ You'll work with a team that truly feels like a community.
 ✅ You'll see the real-world impact of your work every single day.
 ✅ No two days are the same—every day brings a new opportunity to serve, connect, and make a difference.
 We're looking for someone who is passionate, organized, creative, collaborative, and excited to serve families. If that sounds like you—or someone you know—we'd love to see your application!

items.title: Last year, we asked Chattanoogans to invest in the essential services that impact their lives: police, fire, paving, aff...
items.content_text:
Last year, we asked Chattanoogans to invest in the essential services that impact their lives: police, fire, paving, affordable housing, and technology to address traffic congestion. This year's budget is about providing a return on that investment. 
 The approved balanced budget hold's our property tax steady in the face of inflation without cutting services due to a citywide initiative to identify cost savings and hold the line on expenses. The tax rate remains lower than the rate residents pay in Knoxville, Nashville and Memphis. Chattanooga’s tax burden per resident is also lower than Nashville and Knoxville. Swipe to see major investments within the FY27 budget, which begins on July 1.

Please join us in welcoming Dr. Jasmine Fernández to the Chattanooga 2.0 team as our new Director of Adolescent Strategy and Collective Impact!

Dr. Fernández brings a wealth of experience in youth development, education leadership, community organizing, and student support systems. Throughout her career, she has championed efforts that expand opportunity and improve outcomes for young people, ensuring they have the resources, relationships, and guidance needed to thrive.

In this role, she will lead cross-sector efforts to strengthen outcomes for middle and high school youth across Hamilton County, helping align partners, expand access to high-quality opportunities, and support organizations serving young people throughout our community.

We are excited to welcome Dr. Fernández and look forward to the impact her leadership will have on the future of our youth.

#Chattanooga20 #HamiltonCounty #YouthSuccess #CollectiveImpact #EducationLeadership.
